title: Weather-class next-stop feasibility
summary: Separate dry-running and wet-weather-running rows when evaluating feasible next-lap stop options.
rationale: Class-aware fresh-tyre variants were plausible: dry strategy stops differ from wet/intermediate survival decisions. Pirelli’s tyre taxonomy supports treating slicks and wet-weather tyres as different classes.
implementation_hint: Add `Is_Slick`, `Is_WetWeather`, and class-aware next-stop can-finish features: slick options for dry rows, wet/intermediate options for wet-weather rows, and mixed-class switch flags. Include class-specific signed margins.
expected_effect: Small ROC AUC gain with medium confidence if wet-weather rows are present.
risk: Sparse wet-weather examples may make class-specific flags noisy; keep raw compound and general feasibility features.
